column3: Sea Monsters Unmasked & Sea Fables Explained

column3: The Mowing Devil: Or, Strange News out of Hartford-Shire
column14: Earliest known account of a crop circle. A labor dispute ends with a rich farmer telling a mower that the devil would mow his oats before he paid the mower's price; the farmer awakens the next morning to his crops unnaturally mowed.

column3: The Book of Were-Wolves: Being an Account of a Terrible Superstition
column13: Creative nonfiction; Werewolves; Wolves; Arcadia; Mythology, Norse; Folklore; Superstition

column3: Livre de Thot // ou // Collection précieuse des tableaux de la Doctrine de Mercure dans laquelle se trouve le chemin royal de la vie humaine &c &c &c
column13: Tarot; Occultism; Divination; Magic, Egyptian; Fortune-telling
column14: An image of the 78 tarot cards used by Jean-Baptiste Alliette, otherwise known as Etteilla: the occultist who made significant contributions to prescribing tarot with the spiritual, astrological, and divinatory meanings it holds today. His tarot practices held particular sway as the French Revolution began to take shape.
An engraving of a sorceress in the middle of conjuration, surrounded by demonic familiars. It was created during some of the most deadly witch trials that occurred in Europe.
